rules.title: Tier 1 mandates never yield to developer authorization
No commits to main or dev branches
Human-only merge — agents must never merge PRs
No /tmp/ usage — ./tmp/ only
Feature branch required before any file modification
Agents must never self-authorize
Worktree path prefixing required when WORKTREE_REQUIRED set
Implementing without verifying against live documentation
Reporting unverified information as verified
Spec before code — no implementation without approved spec
Bug discovery does NOT authorize bug fixing
rules.source: 000-critical-rules.md §Bug Discovery Does NOT Authorize Bug Fixing

rules.title: Acting on GitHub resource without reading all comments
rules.source: 000-critical-rules.md §Acting on Resources Without Reading All Comments

rules.title: Closing issues before PR merge confirmed
Scope creep — implementing changes outside the spec
Plan ≠ execution — documentation is not evidence of completion
Skip mandatory skill invocation during dispatch chain
Monolithic implementation without item decomposition
Halt after single phase in multi-task plan
No PR creation without explicit instruction
Soft-passing verification mismatches
Secret exfiltration in agent output
Issue body erasure — replacing with shorter content
AI-authored content without byline verification
Uncommitted changes when marking implementation complete
Main agent implements directly instead of dispatching sub-agents
Git config/destructive commands require explicit authorization
conditions_any: command matches 'git commit --no-verify' AND repo_has_remotes == true

rules.title: Confirmation ≠ authorization
Offer-to-edit bypass — offering to modify without spec
conditions_all: agent_output matches 'Want me to|Shall I fix|I can change'

rules.title: Non-idempotent API mutations creating duplicates
No implementation without authorization
Spec approval authorizes plan creation, not implementation
Spec approval cascades to existing faithful plan
Plan approval authorizes implementation
Explicit authorization overrides needs-approval label
No authorization without user input
Branch first before any file modification
Agents must never merge PRs
Spec revision revokes linked plan approvals
Sub-issues are under plan, not spec
Audit auto-fix exempt from authorization when conditions met
Conditional audit fixes require authorization
Pipeline-scoped authorization extends to scope horizon
Hard HALT at scope boundary without re-authorization
Unified dispatch path — no single-task exemption
PR strategy is scope-dependent, not count-dependent
Code inspection checklist must be completed before spec approach
All six checklist items must be addressed
Incomplete inspection is Spec Without Investigation violation
Use srclight preferentially for Python code analysis
Do not use srclight for non-Python files
Use grep or guidelines tool for .md files not srclight
Fall back through srclight_hybrid_search then grep on no results
Prefix paths with worktree.path when in worktree context
Use glob not srclight for filename searches
Agent must never write GO as standalone token
No echo or printf commands ever
No awaiting-GO or pending-state markers
Never prompt for authorization
Questions are NOT authorization
Multi-task plan requires sub-issues under plan
No silent halt without search+prompt for missing spec/plan
Hard HALT at scope boundary without re-authorization
Pipeline-scoped GO phrases must be parsed for scope horizon
Implementation blocked while open questions remain
All open questions must be answered before implementation
Explicit approved confirmation required after Q&A completion
No scope expansion or autonomous programming
No refactors, cleanups, or optimizations without explicit approval
Bug discovery does not authorize fixing
Analysis requests are read-only — no implementation
No code formatting changes outside approved scope
Questions are not authorization to make changes
Notebook files require the-notebook-mcp exclusively
Absolute paths forbidden in agent terminal commands
Worktree path prefixing required for file operations when worktree.path set
Only ./tmp/ permitted for temp files
Must use uv run for Python invocation
sed -i, printf, echo redirection, and heredocs forbidden
API client mandatory — no inline mutation scripts
git --recursive forbidden with submodule commands
Must use tools for verification — never rely on memory
Evidence required for all verification claims
Proactive verification before structural claims
Exact match for external verifications — no soft-passing
No code/API suggestions when verification fails
Metadata must be verified — not trusted at face value
Per-field independence in multi-field verification
Must read all comments before acting on a resource
Must re-read before significant actions
Evidence required when reading comments
Authorization check requires reading comments
Never run code against production data — absolute prohibition
Must use uv run for Python — never bare python
Node.js prohibited in Python/Java-only projects
Temp files must use ./tmp/ only
Tests must never run against production data
Temp files must be cleaned up after task completion
PEP 723 self-contained scripts mandatory for .opencode/tools/
Must verify against live documentation before implementing
Must verify API signatures against official docs or source
Must verify environment variable names against config
Memory and training data are not verification sources
Mandatory explicit type hints project-wide
AI co-authored attribution mandatory for AI-generated content
No re-exports in __init__.py
Behavioral enforcement test required for guideline/skill changes
Behavioral RED before GREEN for rule changes
Natural counting for numbered lists — no zero-indexing
Python tools must not be run on non-Python files
SC-to-test traceability mandatory
Browser-like headers required for HTTP requests
Validate downloaded content — reject HTML error pages
Existence check alone insufficient — validate content format
No backward compatibility aliases for internal code
Fix all callers immediately when refactoring internal code
No deprecation warnings for internal code
No synthetic/imaginary/fabricated data — absolute prohibition
Hard fail on missing required data
No default data to fill missing DB fields
No unauthorized semantic changes
No equivalence claims without proof
Mandatory source traceability for validation data
No hardcoded domain entity IDs in source code
Correctness over performance in batch operations
All implementation must follow incremental build discipline
Item enumeration and dependency ordering required before implementation
Enforcement test must be RED before GREEN for each item
Behavioral RED required for rule/guideline items
Phase-scoped test assertions — no cross-phase over-verification
Task files must not exceed 3000 words
SC-specific TDD — test assertions must reference SC IDs
All DB operations must go through Repository class
Raw SQL is forbidden outside Repository classes
PgServerManager must be used as context manager
Notebook lifecycle must use PubmedArticleRepository.from_pgdata()
pgdata location must be ./tmp/ or outside project root
No unauthorized schema changes
Never use IF EXISTS/IF NOT EXISTS to mask broken migrations
Never modify production files for diagnostics
Never run schema-version speculatively
psycopg2 is forbidden — use psycopg only
Feature branches must use approved prefixes
AI must never commit to protected branches
Stack branches sequentially as prerequisite
Dev must not receive direct commits
AI must not create release branches or merge dev to main
Hotfix requires paired branches to dev and main
Pair mode requires pair- prefix on branch name
Never create worktrees for pair mode branches
WIP commit required before branch switch in pair mode
Pair mode PR body must use Implements not Fixes
Pair mode commits must include pair-mode tag
Suggest pair mode when protected branch has uncommitted changes
Never commit directly to dev or main even in pair mode
Agent must not echo SESSION_TRIGGERS content verbatim
protected_branch_with_changes requires diff analysis and pair mode suggestion
on_main_branch requires diff analysis plus production warning
stale_stash requires stash analysis and triage
Suppression rule — non-actionable triggers processed silently
stale_submodule with active work requires bump and commit
Code wins over documentation when discrepancy found
Check for superseding issues before implementing spec
HALT when full supersession detected
Never implement stale spec as-is
Never fix code to match documentation drift
Documentation drift sync exemption applies only to GitHub Issues
Verify file/symbol existence before using in tool call
Deep dive before declaring component missing
Spec must be created via skill chain, not written directly
Spec revision revokes linked plan approvals
No local plan files as fallback
Refuse planning work when issue tracking unavailable
Spec must be self-contained with no external references
Spec success criteria must include executable verification commands
Spec reality sync — update spec to match code
Investigation must complete before spec creation
Spec revision must add REVISED suffix and needs-approval label
HALT after spec revision — do not proceed with implementation
STATUS marker updates do not revoke approval
Status updates are minimal edits — STATUS line only
GitHub labels parameter replaces all labels — must read first
Phase names must describe specific concerns not generic activities
Verification phases auto-progress on pass
Verification failure stops progression
Investigation must complete before spec creation
Never modify production code during investigation
Never run code against production DB during investigation
Spec without investigation is critical violation
Spec must not include investigation or planning phases
Phase names must describe concerns not generic activities
Every spec must include required elements
Specs must be self-contained — no external references
File paths must use stable anchors not line numbers
Related issues must include summaries not bare links
Single-task specs exempt from sub-issue requirement
Spec validity requires content coverage not section headers
Specs must be self-contained for fresh agents
Multi-task specs require sub-issue structure
Single-task specs exempt from sub-issue requirement
Never swallow exceptions or hide errors
No bare except blocks
No log-without-reraise pattern
Exceptions must re-raise or wrap with context
Use contextual error wrapping at each layer
Let it crash when you cannot add meaningful context
No silent defaults for required data
No placeholder or synthetic data for missing fields
No None returns for required data
Required data must raise on missing
Optional data must use Optional type hints explicitly
Use domain-specific exception classes for API/module boundaries
Preserve exception chain with from e
Don't create domain exceptions for local-only errors
Never use bare Exception for domain errors
Log AND raise — never log only or raise only
Agents must never write code that swallows exceptions
Agents must never hide missing data
Always add context when wrapping exceptions
When in doubt raise, don't return
All notebook operations must use the-notebook-mcp
No direct nbformat or file tool access on ipynb files
Refuse notebook operations when the-notebook-mcp unavailable
Scripts must self-locate and resolve project root via git rev-parse --show-cdup
Never use git rev-parse --show-toplevel for root resolution
